name: Zima Real Bus Line LLC
districts_or_customers: Texas-Mexico intercity routes (Laredo, Houston, Dallas, San Antonio, Austin, Brownsville, McAllen, Edinburg, Eagle Pass; Matehuala, San Luis Potosi, Rio Verde, Celaya)
recent_news: Operates as Autobuses Zima Real, a Texas-Mexico intercity/charter bus line with multiple TX depots and daily service to Mexico; no school-bus footprint identified.
fit_notes: Not a fit for Summit. Zima Real is an intercity/charter motorcoach operator serving the US-Mexico Hispanic travel market; not a yellow school-bus or SPED contractor. Database 'school'=1 flag appears to be a misclassification.

name: Georgia North Bus PDTS Inc
recent_news: No public web footprint located under this name; no website, news, district-contract list, or corporate filing surfaced through standard searches.
fit_notes: Unverifiable from public sources at this time. Address (5834 S 196th St, Kent WA) is a commercial/industrial corridor; if this is a real school-bus subcontractor in the South King County area (Kent/Renton/Federal Way) it could be of interest, but identity, ownership and fleet need primary-source confirmation (WA Secretary of State, UTC, USDOT) before any outreach.

name: Hartnett Transit Svc
recent_news: NJSBCA member school-bus contractor; FMCSA (USDOT 2939241) shows 93 power units, 61 drivers, intrastate-only passenger carrier, MCS-150 refiled Jan 2026, strong safety record (0% driver OOS, 4.6% vehicle OOS).
fit_notes: Solid Summit tuck-in candidate: independent family-owned NJ school-bus contractor with ~90 buses, Howell/Ocean County district relationships, clean safety record, and a named owner (Neil Hartnett). Lakewood/Ocean County is dense school-bus market with succession opportunities; founded 1985 implies ~40-year operator with likely founder transition runway.

name: Breza Bus Svc Inc
recent_news: Family-run NJ school-bus and charter operator (USDOT 872148, MC 680070); fleet of ~31 buses with ~24 drivers per public filings; 70-year-old business still under Breza family control.
fit_notes: Attractive small tuck-in. Multi-generational family operator (founded 1956) in dense Union County NJ market with named principals (Scott and John Breza); fleet ~30 buses fits Summit's small bolt-on profile. Long tenure and family control suggest meaningful succession-driven sale potential.

name: CMR School Bus Co LLC
districts_or_customers: Haverhill MA area (assumed; Haverhill Public Schools transportation page does not name an outsourced contractor)
recent_news: Listed in MA business directories as CMR School Bus Co LLC / CMR School Bus Company LLC at 143 Essex St #6, Haverhill MA; no website, news, ownership, or M&A disclosure found. (The CMR Transport LLC USDOT 3390996 is a separate 1-truck entity in Puyallup WA, not this company.)
fit_notes: Possible small Merrimack Valley MA bolt-on but unverified. Small employee count (~39) and revenue (~$3.6M) imply a sub-30-bus operator; ownership and district contracts unknown. Worth a phone call to (978) 252-0888 and a MA Secretary of State filings pull before any further pursuit; likely too small to justify standalone diligence unless geographic fit (north of Boston, NRT/North Reading Transportation territory) makes it strategic.
